title: C2E2: Chicago Comic and Entertainment Expo at McCormick Place
description: Get geeky at C2E2, Chicago's annual comic and pop culture convention at McCormick Place. Kids might be the most interested in the Family Play Zone, where they can play interactive games, learn to draw their favorite characters, and have fun in the free play zone. Favorite characterswill be there to meet and greet with kids.

title: Warrenville Flashlight Egg Hunt
description: Hunt for eggs after dark at Bower Elementary School in Warrenville. The Easter Bunny will hide special eggs that can be traded for candy at the end of the hunt.

title: West Chicago Glow in the Dark Easter Egg Hunt
description: Hop over to Reed-Keppler Park to search for glow-in-the-dark eggs that can be turned in for prizes. The Easter Bunny will also be hopping around for pictures.
price: $10 per West Chicago resident; $12 per non-resident

title: Vivid Creatures at the Morton Arboretum
description: Vivid Creatures, a new outdoor art exhibition at The Morton Arboretum, is an invitation to explore connections with the natural world through giant, colorful, playful animal sculptures placed outdoors amid towering trees and sweeping green vistas. Each artwork tells a story about the relationships between creatures and the ecosystems where they live.

title: I AM WILD: A Charley Harper Exhibit
description: I AM WILD: A Charley Harper Exhibit explores the flora and fauna of six different habitats, including the Desert, Ocean, Rainforest, Sky, Woods and City. The exhibit invites children and families to discover the diversity and interdependency of life in the natural world, fostering an appreciation for its creatures. Exhibit Features Interactive Habitat Poster Kiosks – Multi-sensory interpretations of Harper’s iconic posters representing each of the featured habitats.

title: The Three Little Pigs at Beverly Arts Center
description: Meet Roxanne, Petunia, and Babe…three Sister Piggies who set out to build their own houses. Roxanne (the Rock’n Roll Pig) builds hers out of Sticks, Petunia (The Pretty Pig) builds hers out of Straw, and Babe (the sensible, smart Pig) builds hers out of bricks! Which house will the Big Bad Wolf Huff and Puff and blow down? Don’t miss CKC’s most popular show!

title: Chicago Theatre Week: Charlie and the Chocolate Factory
description: Based on the beloved novel by Roald Dahl, enjoy this delightfully scrumptious musical adaptation that brings to life all of your favourite characters, songs, and confections. A gobstoppingly good time for the whole family! Grab your golden ticket and join Charlie Bucket on this delectable adventure into Willy Wonka’s world famous chocolate factory!

title: Bunny Bonanza Brunch at Pottawattomie Park
description: Looking for something fun? Pottawattomie Park for Bunny Bonanza Brunch! The event features tattoo stickers, arts & crafts, a delightful brunch with the bunny, an exciting egg hunt, live entertainment & music, the opportunity to capture a picture with the bunny, and the chance to win fantastic prizes. It's a morning packed with kid-friendly fun that you won't want to miss!

title: C2E2: Chicago Comic and Entertainment Expo at McCormick Place
description: Get geeky at C2E2, Chicago's annual comic and pop culture convention at McCormick Place. Kids might be the most interested in the Family Play Zone, where they can play interactive games, learn to draw their favorite characters, and have fun in the free play zone. Favorite characterswill be there to meet and greet with kids.

title: Windy Acres Farm Easter Egg Hunt
description: Discover all that spring and Easter have to offer at Windy Acres Farm! On April 12, 13, 18, and 19, families can enjoy twice-daily Easter Egg hunts, visit with the Easter bunny, flower classes, donut decorating, mining for gems, visits at the petting zoo, and rides on the Choo Choo Express.

title: Bunny Trolley Hop
description: Ride in a historic trolley to visit the Easter Bunny's carrot patch from the Illinois Railway Museum in Union. Kids can meet the Easter Bunny and the live animals in the baby animal zoo before boarding the trolley back to the museum for an Easter egg hunt. The event also includes coloring activities and a food truck.

title: Oak Lawn Annual Easter Egg Hunts
description: The Easter Bunny has hidden eggs all over Oak View Center for kids to find! Children will be separated by age in three egg hunts at 10am, 11am, and 12pm; a craft and a visit from the Easter Bunny follow the egg hunt.
price: $6 for Oak Lawn residents; $12 for non-residents

title: Wonderland Easter Egg Hunt
description: More than 60,000 eggs will be hidden at The Preserve of Highland Park for this Alice in Wonderland themed Easter egg hunt. The hunt will feature scenes and characters from the film among the treat-filled eggs.

title: Bunny Trails Egg Hunt at Euclid Park
description: Gather the family and Euclid Park for an egg hunt, face painting, games and arts & crafts. Please bring your own basket for the Egg Hunt.

title: Bensenville Easter Egg Hunt and Easter Eggstravaganza
description: Hundreds of Easter eggs will be scattered across Varble Park for Bensenville kids to find. Families can gather afterward at the community center for photos with the Easter Bunny, games, crafts, and inflatables.

title: Easter Egg-A-Palooza
description: Hunt for eggs, try your luck at a jelly bean guessing contest, play on inflatables, and have your face painted at Frontier Fieldhouse in Chicago Ridge. Please don't bring your own bag or basket.

title: Cantigny Easter Egg Hunt Open House
description: Kids can find eggs throughout the day in this open format Easter egg hunt. Find five eggs and return them for a prize. Eggs will be empty, so don't try to open or smash them. There is a limit of five eggs and one prize per child. Kids can also enjoy visits with Mr. or Mrs. Easter Bunny, a craft (for a $5 fee), or concessions or brunch.

title: Worth Easter Egg Hunt
description: Terrace Centre in Worth is hosting an Easter egg hunt for kids ages 1 to 12 years old. Registration is required. The event will be held rain or shine.

title: Egg Hunt at Eugene Field Park
description: Preschoolers and kids can enjoy their own egg hunt in this Albany Park park the week before Easter. Bring your own basket! The Easter Bunny will be on hand for photos afterwards.

title: Lombard Egg Hunt
description: Kids are divided into age groups at Lombard Commons to look for Easter eggs, prizes and candy. A visit from the Easter Bunny may also be in store.

title: Bunny-Bash EGG-Stravaganza Melrose Park
description: Perfect for a family day out — snacks, pictures with the Easter Bunny, and the annual egg hunt at Westdale Park in Melrose Park. Kids ages three and under will hunt for eggs at 11:15. Those ages 4-5 will start at 11:45. Kids 6-9 start their egg hunt at 12:15. Kids ages 10-12 can hunt for eggs at 12:45.

title: Egg Hunt at West Lawn Park
description: West Lawn Park hosts an egg hunt for the whole family! Enjoy games, prizes, and pictures with the Easter bunny.

title: Vivid Creatures at the Morton Arboretum
description: Vivid Creatures, a new outdoor art exhibition at The Morton Arboretum, is an invitation to explore connections with the natural world through giant, colorful, playful animal sculptures placed outdoors amid towering trees and sweeping green vistas. Each artwork tells a story about the relationships between creatures and the ecosystems where they live.

title: Budding Botanist
description: On Saturdays and Sundays from 11:00am to 3:00pm, Nature Educators will provide activities for the whole family at The Garfield Park Conservatory's Budding Botanists station. These programs are drop in and no preregistration is required. You can stop by and interact with the materials at the station for as long as you want to. Budding Botanists station will be posted in different areas of the Conservatory on different days. Ask where they are at the front desk! The location will be posted in the lobby each day.

title: I AM WILD: A Charley Harper Exhibit
description: I AM WILD: A Charley Harper Exhibit explores the flora and fauna of six different habitats, including the Desert, Ocean, Rainforest, Sky, Woods and City. The exhibit invites children and families to discover the diversity and interdependency of life in the natural world, fostering an appreciation for its creatures. Exhibit Features Interactive Habitat Poster Kiosks – Multi-sensory interpretations of Harper’s iconic posters representing each of the featured habitats.

title: Atlanta Science Festival
description: The Atlanta Science Festival is a two-week celebration of local science and technology taking place March 7 - 21, 2026, at various locations around Atlanta. Kids explore the world of STEAM with approximately 150+ engaging events.
price: Free; some events require nominal fee

title: Cirque Italia's Water Circus Atlantis in Alpharetta
description: Cirque Italia presents an aquatic spectacular for families at North Point Mall in Alpharetta. The name reflects a unique attribute under the tent, as the show features a custom designed water stage that travels from city to city.

title: Great Big Game Show - Atlanta
description: Great Big Game Show, brought to you by The Escape Game, is an immersive game show experience, located at The Battery in Atlanta. Two teams go head-to-head in a series of classic game show mini-games, on an immersive studio set, with a live host. It’s like all the game shows you love, but this time, you’re the contestant. Play through rounds of strategy, chance, and knowledge, surrounded by spotlights, buzzers, a giant spinning wheel, and a confetti-filled celebration at the end.

title: Easter Egg Quest
description: For almost an entire month, kids search for 50 over-sized eggs around the City of Powder Springs. Families can go anytime during daylight hours to locate the eggs, hidden around Thurman Springs Park and around the downtown area.

title: Atlanta Fair
description: Atlanta's only urban fair is back in the gray lot across from Georgia State's Center Parc Stadium. Families enjoy exciting rides, interactive games and of course, delicious fair food.
city: Gray lot across from Georgia State's stadium
locationName: Gray lot across from Georgia State's stadium
address: Gray lot across from Georgia State's stadium, 688 Central Ave SW, 30315

description: Here's a great outing: spring fun at the Coolray Field Carnival! title: Spring Immersive Walk-Through at FairyTale Village
description: FairyTale Village is an immersive experience for families with young children. In about an hour, you’ll walk through whimsical storybook scenes, explore handcrafted sets filled with magic, light, and sound, and meet the villagers who bring the story to life. This spring, a new tale unfolds. Each year, the FairyTale Villagers work together to grant the wish of one very special creature. This year, it’s a gentle storybook mouse — with the help of a friendly Mushroom Folk and his tiny dragon companion.

title: Super Star Dance for Teens and Adults with Disabilities
description: It’s prom time for people with developmental or physical disabilities and every attendee will be crowned prom king and queen! A DJ will play dance music, food and refreshments are on the menu, and participants will take home a souvenir photo and goody bag.

title: Wing & Rock Fest in Canton
description: The very popular Wing and Rock Festival is back and includes lots of opportunities to taste wings and enjoy live music. There will be a Kids Zone with bouncy houses, a petting zoo, and arts and crafts. There may be an additional fee for the bouncy houses.

title: Milton Community Egg Hunt
description: Kids head to Cambridge High School's football field for a big community egg hunt. The event kicks off with a sensory-friendly egg hunt at 10am. Starting at 11am, the event officially starts and includes games, face painting, music and a visit from the Easter Bunny.

title: Great Big Game Show - Atlanta
description: Great Big Game Show, brought to you by The Escape Game, is an immersive game show experience, located at The Battery in Atlanta. Two teams go head-to-head in a series of classic game show mini-games, on an immersive studio set, with a live host. It’s like all the game shows you love, but this time, you’re the contestant. Play through rounds of strategy, chance, and knowledge, surrounded by spotlights, buzzers, a giant spinning wheel, and a confetti-filled celebration at the end.

title: Orchid Daze at Atlanta Botanical Garden
description: The Atlanta Botanical Garden welcomes guests for Orchid Daze, an annual indoor plant exhibition in the Fuqua Conservatory and Orchid Center, which is scheduled to showcase a variety of orchids including festoons, aerialists, and chandeliers.
price: $24.95-$29.95  for children 3-12; $27.95 - $32.95 for adults (weekday v. weekend pricing)

title: Spring Immersive Walk-Through at FairyTale Village
description: FairyTale Village is an immersive experience for families with young children. In about an hour, you’ll walk through whimsical storybook scenes, explore handcrafted sets filled with magic, light, and sound, and meet the villagers who bring the story to life. This spring, a new tale unfolds. Each year, the FairyTale Villagers work together to grant the wish of one very special creature. This year, it’s a gentle storybook mouse — with the help of a friendly Mushroom Folk and his tiny dragon companion.

title: Stone Mountain Park's Dino Fest
description: Stone Mountain Park’s Dino Fest is back! Dinosaurs roam the grounds and fly overhead. Guests see dinosaurs in 4-D, a new lighted dinosaur-themed parade, plus a special dinosaur Drone & Light Show on Saturday nights.

description: For almost an entire month, kids search for 50 over-sized eggs around the City of Powder Springs. Families can go anytime during daylight hours to locate the eggs, hidden around Thurman Springs Park and around the downtown area.

title: Roswell Dance Starz Presents: "The Greatest Show"
description: Everyone is invited to a spectacular dance production inspired by The Greatest Showman! This high-energy performance celebrates the power of dreams and the magic of the circus with dazzling costumes and electrifying music.

title: Underwater Egg Hunt at Bethesda Park Aquatic Center
description: Splash into the swimming pool at Bethesda Park Aquatic Center to hunt for eggs and earn points. After an hour of indoor swim time, the party moves outdoors with a bounce house, games, crafts, and a Chick-fil-A boxed lunch. Participants can redeem their earned points for prizes. Pre-registration for a time slot is required by March 18, 2026. Timeslots are 9-11am; 10:30am -12:30pm, and Noon to 2pm. Bring a waterproof container for eggs.
price: $21 for Gwinnett County residents; $31 for non-residents

title: Artful Stories at the Michael C. Carlos Museum
description: Children ages 3-6 and their caregivers are invited to the Michael C. Carlos Museum as they read One Small Spark by Ruth Spiro, illustrated by Victoria Tentler-Krylov, an exploration of the Jewish concept of tikkun olam, or repairing the world.
price: $8 per person for members and $12 per person for nonmembers

title: Conyers Cherry Blossom Festival
description: The Conyers Cherry Blossom Festival in Conyers features arts and crafts exhibitors, delicious food, cultural performances, live music, and a children's area! Learn more about beautiful Georgia cherry blossom blooms here!

title: Bunny Brunch at Custer Park Sports and Fitness Center
description: Register for the Bunny Brunch hosted by Marietta Parks and Recreation. Enjoy breakfast items, pictures with the Easter Bunny, crafts, and an Easter egg hunt.

price: $4 - $12 depending on age and Cobb County resident status

description: At the 2nd annual Sneaker Ball families dance, play games, eat, and participate in a dance contest, all in their sneakers! Register online in advance.

description: Families have a blast at the The Acworth Easter Egg Hunt which includes over 50,000 eggs filled with toys, candy and prizes. Activities start at 6:00 pm and kids ages 10 and under may participate. The hunts will take place on the baseball fields; parking will be available at the baseball side of the complex.

title: Flashlight Egg Hunt at Cherokee Veterans Park
description: Families are invited for a unique flashlight egg hunt on Friday evening at Cherokee Veterans Park. A therapeutic hunt will take place on the adjoining field.

title: Great Big Game Show - Atlanta
description: Great Big Game Show, brought to you by The Escape Game, is an immersive game show experience, located at The Battery in Atlanta. Two teams go head-to-head in a series of classic game show mini-games, on an immersive studio set, with a live host. It’s like all the game shows you love, but this time, you’re the contestant. Play through rounds of strategy, chance, and knowledge, surrounded by spotlights, buzzers, a giant spinning wheel, and a confetti-filled celebration at the end.

title: Orchid Daze at Atlanta Botanical Garden
description: The Atlanta Botanical Garden welcomes guests for Orchid Daze, an annual indoor plant exhibition in the Fuqua Conservatory and Orchid Center, which is scheduled to showcase a variety of orchids including festoons, aerialists, and chandeliers.
price: $24.95-$29.95  for children 3-12; $27.95 - $32.95 for adults (weekday v. weekend pricing)

title: Spring Immersive Walk-Through at FairyTale Village
description: FairyTale Village is an immersive experience for families with young children. In about an hour, you’ll walk through whimsical storybook scenes, explore handcrafted sets filled with magic, light, and sound, and meet the villagers who bring the story to life. This spring, a new tale unfolds. Each year, the FairyTale Villagers work together to grant the wish of one very special creature. This year, it’s a gentle storybook mouse — with the help of a friendly Mushroom Folk and his tiny dragon companion.

title: Easter Egg Quest
description: For almost an entire month, kids search for 50 over-sized eggs around the City of Powder Springs. Families can go anytime during daylight hours to locate the eggs, hidden around Thurman Springs Park and around the downtown area.

title: Atlanta Fair
description: Atlanta's only urban fair is back in the gray lot across from Georgia State's Center Parc Stadium. Families enjoy exciting rides, interactive games and of course, delicious fair food.
city: Gray lot across from Georgia State's stadium
locationName: Gray lot across from Georgia State's stadium
address: Gray lot across from Georgia State's stadium, 688 Central Ave SW, 30315

title: LuminoCity Dino Safari at Mall of Georgia
description: Perfect for a family day out — this immersive experience, blending light art with life-sized, roaring dinosaurs and interactive exhibits. Open daily (rain or shine). Enter the festival anytime during your session and stay as long as you want. Children ages 3 and under are FREE for admission. Food available from on-site food trucks.

title: Flight of the Butterflies Exhibit at Fernbank
description: Wander through a kaleidoscope of wonder with Flight of Butterflies, an outdoor exhibit that combines nature, art and storytelling through a showcase of 25 striking butterfly sculptures that line the paths of WildWoods.
price: Value Pricing (Most Weekdays): Adult (13–64) $25.95 | Senior (65+) $24.95 | Child (3–12) $23.95 Standard Pricing (Weekends): Adult (13–64) $27.95 | Senior (65+) $26.95 | Child (3–12) $25.95

title: River Roots Science Stations at Chatthoochee Nature Center
description: To celebrate their 50th anniversary in 2026, Chattahoochee Nature Center is reconnecting to their “River Roots” to explore how people have depended on the river for more than a thousand years.
price: Included with General Admission: $15.09 for children 3-12, $17.24 for 13-18, $21.55 for adults, $17.24 for 65+

title: International Cherry Blossom Festival
description: Head to Macon, Georgia for the ultimate cherry blossom event! This multiday festival features fun rides and games, live nightly concerts, amazing shows and attractions, delicious food and drinks, and of course, everything to celebrate the delicate cherry blossom!

title: Town Center at Cobb Carnival
description: Families head to Town Center at Cobb for tons of rides, games, and of course, carnival food. Unlimited ride wristbands available. Check website for pricing. Wristbands are for persons 36” and over.Ride Heights vary – some require an adult rider with child. RIDE TICKET PRICING: Single ticket $1.5020 tickets $32 ($30 Cash Discount)50 tickets (includes 1 free ride) $62 ($60 Cash Discount)Ride tickets for sale on site daily and online.
price: Free admission and parking; ride prices vary

title: Space Explorers: THE INFINITE Immersive Experience
description: Space Explorers: THE INFINITE is a powerful immersive experience inspired by NASA missions aboard the International Space Station.

title: Aim High: Soaring with the Tuskegee Airmen at Children's Museum of Atlanta
description: AIM HIGH: Soaring with the Tuskegee Airmen explores the multiple aspects of aviation, from the rich history of the Tuskegee Airmen to 21st century STEM concepts.
address: Children's Museum of Atlanta, 275 Centennial Olympic Park Dr NW, 30313

title: Eggcellent Bunny Bonanza  at Honeysuckle Park
description: Spend the morning at Honeysuckle Park in Doraville for an Eggcellent Bunny Bonanza with multiple egg hunts for different age groups, games, photos with celebrity bunnies, and more!

title: Spring Festival  at The Avenue West Cobb
description: Join The Avenue West Cobb for the spring celebration with a festival-style experience designed to be more interactive, relaxed, and enjoyable for the whole family. Instead of a traditional egg hunt, guests can explore a variety of hands-on activities and festive experiences throughout the center. Welcome to Spring Fest: In Bloom—a colorful celebration filled with seasonal décor, playful moments, and opportunities to create sweet springtime memories.

title: Brains On! Live in Atlanta
description: Your favorite science podcast is coming to Atlanta! Hosts Molly Bloom, Marc Sanchez, and Sanden Totten will be there with magic tricks, dance moves, out-of-body experiences, mystery sounds, and even a game show!

title: Brookhaven Cherry Blossom Festival
description: Celebrate the blooming cherry blossoms at Brookhaven's annual festival. The FREE Cherry Blossom Festival returns to Blackburn Park, and features more than 100 artists, as well as kids zone, pet world, a classic car show, delicious food, and more!

title: Hoppy Easter at The Works
description: Hop on over for a fun filled day of exploring a variety of kid-friendly activities, photo opportunities, a petting zoo, and more. Kids’ Egg Hunts begin at 10:30am (1-5 years old) and 11:30am (6-10 years old) at The Camp. Best suited for ages 10 and under.

title: Bluey and Bingo Easter Egg Hunt
description: Everyone is invited to We Rock the Spectrum for an Easter Egg Hunt with the Easter Bunny, Bluey & Bingo! Pencil this in for a fun-filled celebration at the gym featuring:Indoor Easter Egg HuntMeet & Greet with Bluey & BingoSpecial visit from the Easter BunnyMusic, movement & open playToddler-Friendly Time: 10:00 AM – 11:30 AMAges 4 & Up: 12:00 PM – 1:30 PMWe understand that siblings are often of different ages — choose the time slot that works best for your family. Adults are FREE.
price: $30 - first child; Siblings Discounted & Special Pricing for Members

title: Spring Garden Egg-Stravaganza in Lilburn
description: Presented by Live Healthy Gwinnett, this FREE spring festival delights kids with an egg hunt in the community garden at JB Williams Park. Other planned activities feature a bounce house, face painting, games, and cooking demonstrations.
city: J. B. Williams Park Community Garden
locationName: J. B. Williams Park Community Garden

title: Easter Egg Hunt by First Baptist Church of Decatur
description: The Decatur Easter Egg Hunt offers egg hunts for three different age groups, a pop-up market, a special appearance by the Easter Bunny, and more.

title: Eggs-traordinary Extravaganza at Cherokee County Aquatic Center
description: Bring the family out for a fun-filled celebration with underwater egg hunts for kids ages 4-12, an outdoor lawn egg hunt for kids ages 1-3, a bounce house, pics with the Easter Bunny, face painting, games, and more. Slots fill up quickly, so register early. Outside egg hunt (ages 1-3) will be held from 12:15-12:45pm Underwater egg hunts (ages 4-12) will be held at 1pm, 2pm, and 3pm. Sensory-friendly (in-water) egg hunt will be held from 11:30am - 12:15 pm.

title: Easter Egg Hunt at Grant Park
description: Families head to Grant Park for a fun-filled day of hunting for colorful eggs. There will be plenty of eggs to find, each filled with surprises and treats. The Easter Bunny might even make a special appearance, so keep an eye out for some photo opportunities. Don't forget to bring your basket to collect as many eggs as you can!
